q:
This car can only turn to the right.
Does the driver have to turn on the turn signal?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 24 – ‘Obligatory direction’.
The blue sign says the driver must turn to the right.

This car can only turn to the right.

B. No.
clean_e:
LESSON 24 – ‘Obligatory direction’. 
The blue sign says the driver must turn to the right.

q: Do these signs have the same meaning?
e:
LESSON 24 – ‘Obligatory direction’.

clean_e:
LESSON 24 – ‘Obligatory direction’.

q:
There is no roundabout.

e:
LESSON 24 – ‘Obligatory direction’.
No, you have to pass the obstacle right.

He may not turn to the left.

q:
Which traffic sign can replace the blue mandatory sign to obtain the same result?

A. Sign 1.
B. Sign 2.
C. Sign 3.
e:
LESSON 24
1 = Danger sign: succession of dangerous bends.
2 = Prohibition sign: no right turn.
3 = Danger sign: traffic allowed in both directions after a section of the road with one-way traffic.

It is a traffic guide.

q: Must you (the blue car) also give priority to the right, when you drive on a one-way street?
e:
LESSON 24 – ‘Obligatory direction’.
If you're driving on a one-way road, you should also grant priority, except of course when there is a traffic sign indicating you have priority.
In this example, the brown car may drive first.

LESSON 24 – ‘Obligatory direction’.

If the letters P and A are mentioned, it also applies to pedelecs and mopeds class A.

q:
You must turn left at an intersection.
How should you take the turn?

A. As short as possible.
B. As wide as possible.
e:
LESSON 24
- When turning left, you take the turn as wide as possible.
- When turning right, you take the turn as short as possible.
This prevents you from entering the part of the carriageway intended for traffic coming from the opposite direction.

clean_e:

- When turning right, you take the turn as short as possible.
This prevents you from entering the part of the carriageway intended for traffic coming from the opposite direction.
